canonical_question: How do you integrate ERP with demand planning tools like Anaplan, Oracle Demantra, or SAP IBP?
aliases: How to connect ERP historical sales data to Anaplan or SAP IBP for forecasting
Demand forecast to MRP integration pattern
Closed-loop demand planning ERP data pipeline

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: SAP IBP 2408 — CPI-DS replaced by SAP Integration Suite for new implementations (2024-08)

constraints: Forecast granularity mismatch: demand planning tools forecast at product-location-week; ERP MRP typically runs at material-plant-day — aggregation/disaggregation logic is mandatory
Historical data volume: 2-3 years of daily transaction data can exceed 50M rows — batch/bulk APIs required
Consensus review step is human-in-the-loop: no API can replace S&OP meeting sign-off
Frozen forecast horizon: most ERPs lock MRP demand inside a frozen zone (1-4 weeks)
Unit of measure alignment: ERP stores in base UoM, demand planning often uses cases/pallets
Calendar alignment: ERP uses fiscal calendar; demand planning often uses ISO weeks or 4-4-5

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need only SAP IBP API capability details without integration patterns
Need Anaplan API rate limits and authentication specifics only
Need supply chain planning comparison (not integration playbook)

often_confused_with.label: Supply Planning / MRP Integration (downstream of demand planning)

inputs_needed.question: Which ERP system is the source of historical sales data?
options: Oracle ERP Cloud or E-Business Suite

inputs_needed.question: Which demand planning tool are you integrating with?
What integration pattern do you need?
What is your monthly transaction volume?
